title: Pyrus formosensis
taxonomy: Pyrus formosensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the clade Angiosperms, in the subclass Rosidae, in the order Rosales, and in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrus, commonly known as pear trees. This classification suggests a close relationship between Pyrus formosensis and other pear species. Further classification within the genus may be necessary to distinguish it from other Pyrus species. The exact classification of Pyrus formosensis may vary depending on the taxonomic source.
uses: Pyrus formosensis has been traditionally used in Taiwanese medicine due to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that the plant's extracts may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and bark of Pyrus formosensis have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. However, more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant. Traditional healers in Taiwan have used Pyrus formosensis for centuries, and it remains a valuable component of their traditional medicine practices.

preparations: Pyrus formosensis, also known as the Taiwan cherry or Formosa pear, has been used in traditional herbal medicine in Taiwan for centuries. intro: Pyrus formosensis, also known as the Taiwan pear or Formosan pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing fever, improving digestion, and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus formosensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pyrus formosensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and consumed as a fruit or used as a dietary supplement.
